title: Influencer Brand Agreement
description: A campaign-focused sponsorship contract between a brand and an influencer defining deliverables, content guidelines, usage rights, and flat payments.
whatsCovered: Definition of sponsor brand and influencer parties
List of creative deliverables and publishing deadlines
Flat-fee sponsorship payments and payment schedules
Content approval workflow and compliance (FTC tags)
whenToUse: Use this agreement when you are a brand sponsoring an influencer, or a content creator partnering with a company for a sponsored campaign.

fields.label: Brand Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 505 Fitness Way, Los Angeles, CA 90025

fields.label: Influencer Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 12 Venice Beach Dr, Venice, CA 90291

fields.label: Deliverables Description
fields.placeholder: e.g. One (1) dedicated YouTube video review, and two (2) Instagram Stories tagging @brand...

clauses.heading: INFLUENCER MARKETING AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This Influencer Marketing Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {brand_name}, located at {brand_address} ("Sponsor"), and {influencer_name}, located at {influencer_address} ("Creator").

clauses.heading: 1. Services and Deliverables
clauses.body: Creator agrees to create and publish sponsored content for the {campaign_name} campaign. The deliverables shall consist of: {deliverables_desc}. Creator shall submit all drafts to Sponsor for review prior to publication.

clauses.heading: 2. Regulatory and FTC Compliance
clauses.body: Creator shall clearly and conspicuously display sponsorship disclosures in accordance with all Federal Trade Commission (FTC) guidelines, including tags such as '#ad' or '#sponsored' in the top description or video frames.

clauses.heading: 3. Sponsorship Fee
clauses.body: Sponsor agrees to pay Creator a flat fee of {payment_amount} as full compensation for the services. Payments shall be made within 15 days after all deliverables are published and verify compliance.

clauses.heading: 4. Intellectual Property and Licensing
clauses.body: Creator retains ownership of all creative content. Creator hereby grants Sponsor a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual license to repost, share, and use the deliverables for Sponsor's owned media channels.

clauses.heading: 4.1 Exclusivity Period
clauses.body: For a period of 30 days starting from the date of final publication, Creator shall not partner with, post reviews for, or represent any direct competitors of Sponsor in the nutrition/supplement industry.

clauses.heading: 5. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed by, and interpreted under, the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: What is the FTC disclosure requirement?
faq.answer: The FTC requires that you clearly disclose your partnership. Using tags like #ad, #sponsored, or mentioning 'Thanks to brand for sponsoring' is required to remain legal.

faq.question: Can the brand reuse my content?
faq.answer: Yes, under this contract the brand has a non-exclusive license to share and repurpose your posts for their advertising or social media channels, but they do not own your creative assets.
